message.content:
POLARIS MISSION: beat ChatGPT + Gemini + FS-Researcher on deep-research reports.
  DEPTH = process ALL 346 baskets. FAITHFULNESS = context NLI + STRICT numbers.
BINDING INVARIANTS (docs/agentic_outline_redesign.md): faithfulness engine (strict_verify + NLI +
numeric + provenance) is the ONLY hard gate; external content re-enters ONLY via the fold-in seam;
computed numbers render ONLY via the verified [#calc:] lane (never [CITE:ev_xxx]); exploratory
execute_python is BARRED from rendering; weight-and-consolidate, never filter.
FENCE: work ONLY inside /home/polaris/wt/compose_fix (branch bot/compose-fix). NEVER touch another worktree or the
operator's /home/polaris/polaris_project monitoring dir. Report REAL output only; never fake a pass.


YOU ARE THE BUILDER (Opus hands) for "compose". Fable already found the problems and
told you EXACTLY what to change. DO NOT re-investigate, DO NOT hunt for an entrypoint, DO NOT wander.
Apply ONLY these fixes to src/providers/llm_provider.py, in order:
[
  {
    "severity": "P1",
    "file_line": "src/providers/llm_provider.py:149 and src/polaris_graph/generator/multi_section_generator.py:4993-4996",
    "problem": "get_semaphore() rebinds the module-global asyncio.Semaphore whenever the running loop differs (VERIFIED: `if _LLM_SEMAPHORE is None or _LLM_SEMAPHORE_LOOP is not running_loop` rebuilds at full cap), and _redraft creates a fresh ThreadPoolExecutor + `asyncio.run(_run())` per re-draft — a brand-new loop each time — so main-loop/redraft-loop alternation destroys the semaphore ~300x/run, in-flight permits vanish, and PG_MAX_CONCURRENT_LLM bounds nothing (unbounded bursts feed the 429/connection-error storms). Note the judge transport bypasses this semaphore entirely (entailment_judge builds its own httpx client), so the judge cap (fix #2) is the only judge bound. (Dedups 2 findings.)",
    "fix": "In llm_provider.py: key semaphores per-loop in a WeakKeyDictionary[loop -> Semaphore] instead of clobbering one global. In multi_section_generator._redraft: reuse one persistent worker loop (or make _redraft async and await _call_writer on the caller's loop) instead of asyncio.run per call. Keep the blocking fut.result off the event loop (guaranteed once fix #1 lands).",
    "acceptance": "'Concurrency semaphore (re)initialized' log line appears once per distinct loop (<=2-3 per run), not 300+; instrumented in-flight OpenRouterClient call count never exceeds PG_MAX_CONCURRENT_LLM across a full run with redrafts."
  }
]

For each: make the concrete change at the file:line; then ACTUALLY EXERCISE the touched path (run it,
py_compile is NOT enough — the acceptance observable tells you what to run); then git commit in
/home/polaris/wt/compose_fix with an honest message (say PARTIAL if partial). Append one dated line to
/home/polaris/wt/compose_fix/WHEEL_PROGRESS.md. Report what landed vs what Fable asked. A skipped fix reported is fine; hidden is not.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	"""
2	LLM Provider Abstraction Layer (A7.3 + A8.2).
3	
4	Provides:
5	- ABC-based LLMProvider interface with OpenRouter, vLLM, and Ollama backends
6	- Global concurrency semaphore preventing GPU/API OOM crashes (A8.2)
7	- Exponential backoff with jitter for rate limit / overload recovery
8	- Sovereign mode toggle: PG_SOVEREIGN_MODE=1 forces local-only providers
9	- Factory function get_llm_provider() returns the correct provider
10	
11	All polaris_graph pipeline LLM calls go through OpenRouterClient directly
12	(battle-tested, 1617 lines). This module provides:
13	1. Configuration resolution (which provider, which model)
14	2. Global concurrency control wrapping ALL LLM calls
15	3. Sovereign mode enforcement
16	4. Provider validation
17	"""
18	
19	import asyncio
20	import logging
21	import os
22	import random
23	from abc import ABC, abstractmethod
24	from dataclasses import dataclass
25	from typing import Optional
26	
27	from dotenv import load_dotenv
28	
29	load_dotenv()
30	
31	logger = logging.getLogger("polaris.providers.llm")
32	
33	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
34	# Configuration from environment (LAW VI)
35	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
36	
37	# Provider selection
38	LLM_PROVIDER = os.getenv("POLARIS_LLM_PROVIDER", "openrouter")
39	
40	# Sovereign mode: when enabled, ALL external API calls are blocked
41	PG_SOVEREIGN_MODE = os.getenv("PG_SOVEREIGN_MODE", "0") == "1"
42	
43	# OpenRouter (cloud)
44	OPENROUTER_BASE_URL = os.getenv("OPENROUTER_BASE_URL", "https://openrouter.ai/api/v1")
45	OPENROUTER_API_KEY = os.getenv("OPENROUTER_API_KEY", "")
46	OPENROUTER_MODEL = os.getenv("OPENROUTER_MODEL", "deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro")
47	
48	# vLLM (local GPU server)
49	VLLM_BASE_URL = os.getenv("VLLM_BASE_URL", "http://localhost:8200/v1")
50	# B10 (2026-06-14): repointed off the gemma default. This is the DORMANT sovereign
51	# vLLM path (only get_semaphore is imported into pipeline-A, so this default never
52	# reaches the live faithfulness path), but a google/gemma-* default here pins gemma
53	# into the sovereign stack against the locked stack
54	# (config/architecture/polaris_runtime_lock.yaml: mirror/evaluator = z-ai/glm-5.1,
55	# NO gemma) and trips the verify_lock conformance gate. The default is the locked
56	# GLM-5.1 slug; the operator's sovereign box must serve a GLM-5.1 weight at this
57	# endpoint (the exact local HF/AWQ artifact is deployment-specific — override
58	# VLLM_MODEL with the served path on the sovereign box). LAW VI: env-overridable.
59	VLLM_MODEL = os.getenv("VLLM_MODEL", "z-ai/glm-5.1")
60	VLLM_API_KEY = os.getenv("VLLM_API_KEY", "EMPTY")
61	
62	# Ollama (local, lightweight)
63	OLLAMA_BASE_URL = os.getenv("OLLAMA_BASE_URL", "http://localhost:11434/v1")
64	# I-cd-010 / GH#625: Ollama legacy fallback; not active under the Carney
65	# demo vLLM lock (Gemma 4 31B-it via ebircak/...AWQ on the evaluator box).
66	OLLAMA_MODEL = os.getenv("OLLAMA_MODEL", "llama3.1:70b")
67	
68	# Local embedding model (sovereign mode)
69	LOCAL_EMBEDDING_MODEL = os.getenv(
70	    "PG_LOCAL_EMBEDDING_MODEL", "BAAI/bge-large-en-v1.5"
71	)
72	
73	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
74	# A8.2: Global Concurrency Semaphore
75	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
76	
77	# Max concurrent LLM requests across ALL pipeline nodes
78	# Cloud: 5 (API rate limits), GPU: 3 (VRAM constraints)
79	_MAX_CONCURRENT_LLM = int(os.getenv("PG_MAX_CONCURRENT_LLM", "5"))
80	
81	# Retry configuration
82	_LLM_RETRY_MAX = int(os.getenv("PG_LLM_RETRY_MAX", "3"))
83	_LLM_RETRY_BASE_DELAY = float(os.getenv("PG_LLM_RETRY_BASE_DELAY", "1.0"))
84	
85	# GPU memory threshold for auto-throttle (sovereign mode)
86	_GPU_MEMORY_THRESHOLD = float(os.getenv("PG_GPU_MEMORY_THRESHOLD", "0.90"))
87	
88	# Singleton semaphore — initialized lazily, KEYED to the event loop it was
89	# created in. A module-global asyncio.Semaphore binds to the loop that first
90	# touched it; under the Gate-B `--all` cert run each question is driven by its
91	# own asyncio.run() (a FRESH loop per query), so a semaphore created in query 1's
92	# loop and reused in query 2 raises "bound to a different event loop" and kills
93	# Q2..Q5 (F01 / A3 master fix list). We therefore store the loop the semaphore
94	# belongs to and recreate it whenever the running loop differs.
95	_LLM_SEMAPHORE: Optional[asyncio.Semaphore] = None
96	_LLM_SEMAPHORE_LOOP: Optional[asyncio.AbstractEventLoop] = None
97	_SEMAPHORE_LOCK = asyncio.Lock() if hasattr(asyncio, "Lock") else None
98	
99	
100	def _max_concurrent_llm() -> int:
101	    """Resolve the concurrency cap at CALL time (LAW VI / F16).
102	
103	    Reads ``PG_MAX_CONCURRENT_LLM`` from the live environment, falling back to
104	    the import-time module default. This lets the Gate-B full-capability slate
105	    (which mutates ``os.environ`` AFTER this module is imported) set the cap
106	    without a stale import-time freeze — the same class of fix as
107	    ``set_max_cost_per_run``. A malformed value falls back to the default
108	    rather than crashing the run. Default unchanged ⇒ byte-identical when unset.
109	    """
110	    raw = os.getenv("PG_MAX_CONCURRENT_LLM")
111	    if raw is None:
112	        return _MAX_CONCURRENT_LLM
113	    try:
114	        value = int(raw)
115	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
116	        logger.warning(
117	            "[llm_provider] PG_MAX_CONCURRENT_LLM=%r is not an int; "
118	            "falling back to default %d", raw, _MAX_CONCURRENT_LLM,
119	        )
120	        return _MAX_CONCURRENT_LLM
121	    return value if value > 0 else _MAX_CONCURRENT_LLM
122	
123	
124	def get_semaphore() -> asyncio.Semaphore:
125	    """Get or create the global LLM concurrency semaphore for the CURRENT loop.
126	
127	    Lazy initialization, rebound per event loop. The semaphore limits the number
128	    of concurrent LLM API calls across all pipeline nodes to prevent:
129	    - 429 rate limit errors on cloud APIs
130	    - GPU OOM crashes on local vLLM deployments
131	
132	    F01 (A3): an ``asyncio.Semaphore`` is bound to the loop it is created in.
133	    The Gate-B ``--all`` run drives each question with a separate ``asyncio.run``
134	    (a fresh loop), so reusing a query-1 semaphore in query 2 raises
135	    ``RuntimeError: ... bound to a different event loop``. We detect the running
136	    loop and recreate the semaphore whenever it differs (or no loop was recorded),
137	    so every query gets a semaphore on its own loop. The check + set has no
138	    ``await`` between them, so it is race-free within a single loop.
139	    """
140	    global _LLM_SEMAPHORE, _LLM_SEMAPHORE_LOOP
141	    try:
142	        running_loop: Optional[asyncio.AbstractEventLoop] = (
143	            asyncio.get_running_loop()
144	        )
145	    except RuntimeError:
146	        # No running loop (e.g. a sync caller); fall back to the legacy
147	        # lazy-singleton behavior so the semaphore is still usable.
148	        running_loop = None
149	    if _LLM_SEMAPHORE is None or _LLM_SEMAPHORE_LOOP is not running_loop:
150	        cap = _max_concurrent_llm()
151	        _LLM_SEMAPHORE = asyncio.Semaphore(cap)
152	        _LLM_SEMAPHORE_LOOP = running_loop
153	        logger.info(
154	            "[llm_provider] Concurrency semaphore (re)initialized: max=%d loop=%r",
155	            cap, running_loop,
156	        )
157	    return _LLM_SEMAPHORE
158	
159	
160	def reset_semaphore() -> None:
161	    """Reset the global semaphore (for testing or config changes)."""
162	    global _LLM_SEMAPHORE, _LLM_SEMAPHORE_LOOP
163	    _LLM_SEMAPHORE = None
164	    _LLM_SEMAPHORE_LOOP = None
165	
166	
167	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
168	# A8.2: Exponential Backoff with Jitter
169	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
170	
171	
172	class RateLimitError(Exception):
173	    """Raised when the API returns a rate limit response (429)."""
174	
175	
176	class ServerOverloadError(Exception):
177	    """Raised when the API returns a server overload response (503)."""
178	
179	
180	async def retry_with_backoff(fn, max_retries: int = _LLM_RETRY_MAX,
181	                             base_delay: float = _LLM_RETRY_BASE_DELAY):
182	    """Retry an async callable with exponential backoff + random jitter.
183	
184	    Retries on RateLimitError, ServerOverloadError, and TimeoutError.
185	    Other exceptions propagate immediately.
186	
187	    Args:
188	        fn: Async callable (no arguments) to retry.
189	        max_retries: Maximum number of retry attempts.
190	        base_delay: Base delay in seconds (doubles each attempt).
191	
192	    Returns:
193	        The result of fn() on success.
194	
195	    Raises:
196	        The last exception if all retries are exhausted.
197	    """
198	    last_exc = None
199	    for attempt in range(max_retries + 1):
200	        try:

4920	    from src.polaris_graph.generator.verified_compose import (  # noqa: PLC0415
4921	        build_short_member_sentence as _llm_repair_short_writer,
4922	    )
4923	    return _repair_untokened_draft(
4924	        rewritten, baskets, evidence_pool,
4925	        writer_fn=lambda _b, _p: _llm_repair_short_writer(_b, evidence_pool),
4926	        verify_fn=_llm_repair_verify,
4927	    )
4928	
4929	
4930	def _make_group_redraft_fn(
4931	    evidence_pool: dict, section_context: "dict | None" = None,
4932	) -> "Callable[..., str]":
4933	    """I-deepfix-001 Wave-1a (#1344): build the SYNTH_PRIMARY group-mode re-draft closure threaded into
4934	    ``_compose_section_per_basket`` -> ``_compose_one_basket``'s bounded repair loop. A SYNC callable
4935	    ``(basket, scoped_pool, *, revise_reasons=None) -> str`` that re-calls the async GROUP writer
4936	    (``group_mode=True``) with the fed-back wrapper failure reasons and returns a FRESH draft string; the
4937	    caller re-verifies it with the UNCHANGED verify_fn (this closure NEVER verifies, NEVER relaxes a
4938	    gate). ``_compose_section_per_basket`` runs synchronously inside the already-running section event
4939	    loop, so the async writer call is executed in an ISOLATED worker-thread event loop (its own
4940	    ``asyncio.run``) and joined — safe to invoke from inside the running loop. Fail-open: any error /
4941	    empty draft returns "" and the bounded repair loop simply keeps the prior draft (never crashes the
4942	    section, never ships a failed authored sentence — the exhaustion path renders the labeled K-span).
4943	    No new module-level import on the hot path: abstractive_writer + its config are imported inline
4944	    behind the flag."""
4945	    from src.polaris_graph.generator.abstractive_writer import (  # noqa: PLC0415
4946	        _DEFAULT_CALL_DEADLINE_S,
4947	        _DEFAULT_MAX_TOKENS,
4948	        _DEFAULT_REASONING_MAX_TOKENS,
4949	        _DEFAULT_TEMPERATURE,
4950	        _ENV_CALL_DEADLINE_S,
4951	        _ENV_MAX_TOKENS,
4952	        _ENV_REASONING_MAX_TOKENS,
4953	        _ENV_TEMPERATURE,
4954	        _call_writer,
4955	        _env_float,
4956	        _env_int,
4957	        _resolve_model,
4958	    )
4959	    from src.polaris_graph.generator.verified_compose import (  # noqa: PLC0415
4960	        _basket_supports_members,
4961	    )
4962	
4963	    model = _resolve_model()
4964	    max_tokens = max(1, _env_int(_ENV_MAX_TOKENS, _DEFAULT_MAX_TOKENS))
4965	    reasoning_max_tokens = max(0, _env_int(_ENV_REASONING_MAX_TOKENS, _DEFAULT_REASONING_MAX_TOKENS))
4966	    temperature = _env_float(_ENV_TEMPERATURE, _DEFAULT_TEMPERATURE)
4967	    call_deadline_s = max(1.0, _env_float(_ENV_CALL_DEADLINE_S, _DEFAULT_CALL_DEADLINE_S))
4968	
4969	    def _redraft(basket: Any, _scoped_pool: dict, *, revise_reasons: "list[str] | None" = None) -> str:
4970	        members = _basket_supports_members(basket)
4971	        if not members:
4972	            return ""
4973	
4974	        async def _run() -> str:
4975	            return await asyncio.wait_for(
4976	                _call_writer(
4977	                    members, evidence_pool,
4978	                    model=model, max_tokens=max_tokens,
4979	                    reasoning_max_tokens=reasoning_max_tokens, temperature=temperature,
4980	                    revise_reasons=revise_reasons, group_mode=True,
4981	                    section_context=section_context,
4982	                ),
4983	                timeout=call_deadline_s,
4984	            )
4985	
4986	        import concurrent.futures as _futures  # noqa: PLC0415
4987	        # Codex P0 / Fable P1: BOUND fut.result() with a wall (call_deadline_s + grace). A writer call
4988	        # wedged in httpx client teardown (the proven hang class, abstractive_writer.py teardown-drain)
4989	        # would make an UNBOUNDED .result() block the whole run forever — and a `with` context manager
4990	        # would then join that wedged worker at __exit__ and re-wedge. So DO NOT use the context manager:
4991	        # on a wall breach ABANDON the worker (shutdown(wait=False) — a bounded 1-thread leak, mirroring
4992	        # the pre-pass abandon semantics) and return "" so the bounded repair loop keeps the prior draft.
4993	        _ex = _futures.ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=1)
4994	        fut = _ex.submit(lambda: asyncio.run(_run()))
4995	        try:
4996	            result = str(fut.result(timeout=call_deadline_s + 30.0) or "")
4997	            _ex.shutdown(wait=False)
4998	            return result
4999	        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — timeout/wedge/error => ABANDON worker, keep prior draft (fail-open)
5000	            _ex.shutdown(wait=False)
5001	            logger.warning(
5002	                "[multi_section] SYNTH_PRIMARY group re-draft failed/timed-out (non-fatal) -> keep prior "
5003	                "draft (worker abandoned)", exc_info=True,
5004	            )
5005	            return ""
5006	
5007	    return _redraft
5008	
5009	
5010	# ── I-deepfix-001 Wave-2d (#1344) — TWO-SIDED DEBATE disclosure ───────────────────────────────────
5011	#
5012	# DeepTRACE One-Sided (#1) / Overconfident (#2): a DEBATE-framed section ("benefits and risks",
5013	# "pros and cons", "positive vs negative views") that renders only the majority (pro) side scores
5014	# one-sided. The con side is already PLACED by existing machinery — B1
5015	# (``PG_DEBATE_CON_BASKET_CONSOLIDATION``, default-ON) consolidates the refuter con-basket into the
5016	# section compose set (``_section_baskets_for_compose``) and ``_compose_section_per_basket`` composes
5017	# every basket per-clause-verified against its OWN basket-scoped pool. Wave-2d never composes a con
5018	# clause; it INSPECTS the composed units and, for a debate-framed section that carries a verified PRO
5019	# clause but NO verified CON clause (the con side genuinely absent / unverifiable), appends an HONEST
5020	# marker-less asymmetry disclosure. It NEVER fabricates a con, NEVER asserts an ungrounded balancing
5021	# claim (under-relax is safe; fabricating balance is the lethal direction). The faithfulness engine is
5022	# byte-untouched — the disclosure renders after strict_verify (``render_degraded_disclosures``), is
5023	# never verified prose, never counted as support. Default OFF (``PG_TWO_SIDED_DEBATE``) => no-op =>
5024	# byte-identical.
5025	_TWO_SIDED_DEBATE_ENV = "PG_TWO_SIDED_DEBATE"
5026	_TWO_SIDED_DEBATE_DISCLOSURE_PREFIX = "[no verified counter-evidence"
5027	# The evidence_id inside a ``[#ev:<id>:<start>-<end>]`` provenance token (the shape strict_verify emits).
5028	_EV_ID_IN_TOKEN_RE = re.compile(r"\[#ev:([A-Za-z0-9_]+):\d+-\d+\]")
5029	
5030	
5031	def _two_sided_debate_enabled() -> bool:
5032	    """``PG_TWO_SIDED_DEBATE`` kill-switch (default OFF, LAW VI). OFF => the debate disclosure pass is
5033	    never entered => byte-identical."""
5034	    return os.getenv(_TWO_SIDED_DEBATE_ENV, "0").strip().lower() not in ("", "0", "false", "off", "no")

588	async def _call_writer(
589	    members: list,
590	    evidence_pool: dict,
591	    *,
592	    model: str,
593	    max_tokens: int,
594	    reasoning_max_tokens: int,
595	    temperature: float,
596	    revise_reasons: Optional[list[str]] = None,
597	    group_mode: bool = False,
598	    catch_transport: bool = False,
599	    section_context: "dict | None" = None,
600	) -> str:
601	    """ONE LLM writer call for a basket: rephrase the SUPPORTS members' verified spans into clean
602	    declarative prose carrying the canonical tokens. Returns the raw draft text (re-verified by the
603	    unchanged compose loop). On any error returns "" -> the loop falls back to the K-span (fail-loud
604	    to the K-span, never a silent crash).
605	
606	    I-deepfix-001 Wave-1a (#1344): ``group_mode`` selects the GROUP writer contract
607	    (``_WRITER_SYSTEM_GROUP`` + the connected-paragraph lead) — one coherent multi-sentence narrative
608	    over the whole basket's spans instead of one sentence per span. Default False => byte-identical.
609	
610	    I-deepfix-001 B3 (#1370): ``catch_transport`` (set only by the transport-aware pre-pass branch)
611	    catches the httpx transport-DISCONNECT family — ConnectTimeout / ConnectError / RemoteProtocolError
612	    / ReadError — and returns the ``_WRITER_TRANSPORT_DISCONNECT`` sentinel instead of letting the
613	    exception escape as an unretrieved-task leak ("Task exception was never retrieved"). Default False =>
614	    NO catch => byte-identical (the exception propagates exactly as before, K-span via the caller)."""
615	    import httpx  # noqa: PLC0415 — local import (transport-disconnect classes; B3 catch only)
